name: Copperbelt Energy Corporation PLC
description: Copperbelt Energy Corporation PLC (CEC) is a Zambian power utility listed on the LuSE Main Board since 2008. CEC generates, transmits, distributes, and supplies electricity primarily to mining companies on the Copperbelt and in the DRC. The company is executing a major renewable energy expansion strategy through its subsidiary CEC Renewables, including solar PV plants and Green Bond issuances. In 2024, CEC commissioned its second solar PV plant at Itimpi (60 MWp) and issued a US$96.7 million second tranche Green Bond.

topHolders.name: ZCCM-IH (Strategic Stakeholder)
topHolders.note: Historical strategic interest; exact stake unconfirmed

dividend.note: 8.8% dividend increase declared for 2024 (per Chairperson's letter)

greenInitiatives.greenBonds: US$53M (2023) + US$96.7M (2024 tranche 2) via CEC Renewables

analystNotes.note: CEC's 43% revenue growth to ZMW 15.83B is impressive, driven by regional power sales. However, the 30% decline in PAT (USD 96.9M in USD terms, ZMW 2.82B) reflects one-off items: KCM receivable writeback in prior year and Kabompo hydropower impairment. Normalized earnings are stronger than headline suggests. The Green Bond strategy and solar expansion position CEC well for Zambia's open-access energy reforms. FCF of only ZMW 81M on ZMW 15.8B revenue is concerning — heavy capex cycle underway.
analystNotes.author: Vituli Musukuma, PhD

riskFactors: Heavy capex cycle — ZMW 2.38B in capital expenditures consuming nearly all operating cash flow
Mining sector dependency — customer concentration risk on Copperbelt and DRC mines
Hydropower vulnerability — drought and load management affected operations in 2024
Green Bond debt obligations — US$149.7M total across two tranches
Currency risk — USD-denominated revenues and debt, ZMW functional currency
investmentThesis: CEC is a utility-in-transition: pivoting from hydropower-dependent transmission to a diversified renewables generator. The 43% revenue growth and expanding DRC market signal strong demand. However, the heavy capex cycle means minimal free cash flow for the near term. The 4.5% dividend yield is modest for the risk. The investment case hinges on execution of the solar pipeline and whether open-access energy reforms unlock new revenue streams. Worth monitoring for entry after capex peak.
stance: HOLD — MONITOR CAPEX CYCLE & SOLAR EXECUTION
dataSources: CEC FY2024 Abridged Financials — LuSE SENS (Mar 2025)
dataQuality: HIGH — Based on audited FY2024 annual report, integrated report, and verified market data
